**Client:** Yes, na so. Dem break into my shop last week, carry some things go. I don even go report am for station dat day, but I no collect any paper. Na my insurance agent now say I need the official report.

**Agent:** Ah, sorry to hear dat, Chike. No wahala, I go explain the process for you. Since you don already report am for station, the first step na for you to go back to dat same police station where you first report the incident.

**Client:** Okay, I get my ID card and I fit remember the date I go. My insurance company no give me letter yet, dem say make I bring the report first.

**Agent:** No problem. When you reach the station, ask for the officer in charge of reports, or the IPO (Investigating Police Officer) wey dey handle your case. Explain say you need a copy of the police extract or report for your insurance claim. Dem go fit give you a form to fill, or prepare the official report for you based on the information you already gave dem. Sometimes, dem fit charge small administrative fee, but generally, the report itself no suppose cost much.

**Client:** So I just go there, tell dem say I need the report for insurance, and dem go give me form?

**Agent:** Precisely. Make sure you follow up politely. If dem tell you to come back, ask for a specific date and time, and the name of the officer you should meet. E don clear for you so far?

**Client:** Yes, Funke, e don clear well well. Thank you so much, you don help me a lot. I for just dey confuse.

**Agent:** You are welcome, Chike. Dat's why we dey here. Anything else I fit help you with today?

**Agent:** Alright, Chike. Have a good day.

Client: Ah, okay. So, I just go to the station with my ID and anything wey happen for the incident. Then dem go give me the form to fill?
Agent: Exactly. You go explain the incident to the officer on duty, dem go give you a form to fill, then dem go process your report. E fit take one or two days to get the final stamped copy, depending on how busy dem dey.
Client: Ah, okay, okay. Thank you very much, Officer Tunde. You don help me well well.
Agent: You are welcome, Mr. John. Is there anything else I fit help you with today?
Client: No, dat na all for now. Thank you.
Agent: Alright. Have a good day, Mr. John. Remember, for emergencies, always dial 112.
